description: A transitive dependency ships with no resolvable license and is waved through because the top-level package looked fine.
body: Failure mode: the build resolves the top-level package's license but a deep transitive dependency carries NOASSERTION or no LICENSE file, and it ships anyway because nobody walked the full tree. Detection signals: the scanner reports NOASSERTION/unknown for a component; the inventory row count is less than the lockfile component count; only the root license was checked. Do not assume the root license covers transitive dependencies or treat unknown as permissive. Safer fallback: walk the resolved lockfile, resolve every component to an SPDX identifier from its bundled LICENSE text, and block release on any remaining unknown until a human resolves it.
